visa_policy.notes: Russia's e-visa (unified electronic visa) is available to citizens of 68 countries as listed by the Russian Government on the official kdmid.ru portal. Validity is 120 days from issue date; permitted stay up to 30 days; single-entry; processed in 4 calendar days; fee USD 52 (children under 6 exempt). Visa-free countries are based on bilateral agreements. RuID digital pre-registration: from June 30, 2025, Russia launched an experimental electronic notification system for visa-free travelers; registration via Gosuslugi portal or RuID app is VOLUNTARY during the experiment (until Dec 31, 2027) per official Ministry of Digital Development clarifications — NOT mandatory as previously stated. EAEU members (Armenia, Belarus, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan) have free movement of labor. Starting Jan 1, 2025, EAEU citizens face a 90-day per calendar year cap without additional documents. China individual visa-free (30 days) is active Dec 1, 2025 – Sep 14, 2026 under Presidential Executive Order. Jordan full ordinary-passport visa-free (30 days) entered force Dec 13, 2025.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of any valid Belarus visa (not e-visa) plus a mutually recognized identity document may enter Russia during the validity period of that visa. Period of stay calculated from date of entry into first country from a third state. Land border crossing restricted to 6 designated routes: Yukhovichi-Dolostsy, Jezerishche-Nevel, Liozno-Kruglovka, Redki-Krasnaya Gorka, Zvenchatka-Dubovichka, Selishche-Novozybkov. E-visas excluded pending settlement of technical aspects. Agreement in force since January 11, 2025.

credential.subtype: temporary residence permit or permanent residence permit (Belarus-issued)

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of a valid Belarus temporary or permanent residence permit plus a mutually recognized identity document may enter Russia during the validity period of the permit. Period of stay calculated from date of entry into Russia from a third state. Land border crossing restricted to 6 designated routes (same as Belarus visa holders). E-visas excluded. Agreement in force since January 11, 2025; agreed under Agreement between the Government of the Republic of Belarus and the Government of the Russian Federation on mutual recognition of visas, signed June 19, 2020.

credential.subtype: APEC Business Travel Card with RUS code on reverse

conditional_access.conditions: Foreign nationals holding an APEC Business Travel Card (physical card only — virtual ABTC requires a special multiple-entry permit) with 'RUS' code on the back side may enter and exit Russia without a visa on the basis of the card plus a valid identity document recognized by Russia. Permitted for business trips only. Maximum 90 days per any 180-day period. Priority lane access at immigration checkpoints. Russia became a full APEC participant on June 1, 2013. Governing decree: Presidential Decree No. 777 of June 5, 2012. Eligible cardholders are business persons approved by APEC economies: Australia, Brunei, Chile, China, Hong Kong, Indonesia, Japan, South Korea, Malaysia, Mexico, New Zealand, Papua New Guinea, Peru, Philippines, Singapore, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam and other full APEC members.

credential.subtype: Alaska indigenous tribal member status with relative in Chukotka

conditional_access.conditions: Residents of Alaska who are members of indigenous populations (tribal members) and have blood relatives, tribal members, or persons sharing linguistic/cultural heritage in Chukotka Autonomous Okrug may enter Chukotka without a visa when invited by a relative. Entry limited to four designated checkpoints: Anadyr, Provideniya, Lavrentiya, and Uelen. Agreement ratified July 17, 2015. The broader arrangement dates to 1989 (indigenous peoples of the Bering Strait region). Access is restricted to Chukotka Autonomous Okrug only.

Holders of diplomatic, official, service, and/or special passports of numerous countries (100+) may enter Russia without a visa under bilateral agreements. Stay is typically 90 days per 180-day period (or 3 months per period). Specific countries and permitted passport types (D=diplomatic, O=official, S=service, Sp=special) vary by bilateral treaty. Notable exceptions: visa-free exemption for diplomatic/official passport holders of EU member states, Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, and Switzerland has been suspended. Israel diplomatic and service passport holders require a visa. Belarus and South Ossetia have freedom of movement. Precise per-country conditions are governed by individual bilateral agreements listed on the Russian MFA portal.

Lebanon and Russia signed a visa waiver agreement on April 22, 2026 exempting holders of diplomatic, service, and special passports from entry visa requirements. Signed at the Foreign Ministry headquarters in Beirut by Russian Ambassador Alexander Rudakov and Lebanon's Foreign Ministry secretary-general Abdel Sattar Issa. Agreement enters into force 60 days after exchange of ratification notifications; remains valid for five years and automatically renewed unless suspended. Stay limits not specified in available text. NOTE: Agreement signed but NOT YET IN FORCE as of validation date.

cbi.notes: Russia does not have a formal citizenship-by-investment (CBI) program. Citizenship can be pursued after 5 years of permanent residency. The investor/golden visa (Government Decree No. 2573, Dec 31, 2022) grants permanent residence directly but does not grant citizenship. No official CBI program exists.

programs.name: Russian Golden Visa (Investor Residence Permit)
programs.notes: Established by Government Decree No. 2573 (Dec 31, 2022), effective Jan 11, 2023. Grants direct permanent residence (not temporary residence first). Investment options: (1) RUB 15 million in socially significant regional projects within 3 years; (2) RUB 30 million in a Russian company operating 3+ years and paying RUB 6 million/year in taxes; (3) Registration of a Russian commercial entity with RUB 4 million/year taxes paid, operating 2+ years; (4) Real estate investment with cadastral value: RUB 50 million (Moscow), RUB 20 million (Far East), RUB 25 million (elsewhere) — new build under 2 years old, held 1+ year before applying. Total annual quotas: 3,802 for 2026. Requirement to stay 6 months/year in Russia was removed in late 2025/early 2026.

programs.name: Shared Values Visa (Temporary Residence Permit)
programs.notes: Presidential Decree No. 702 (Aug 19, 2024). Grants temporary residence permit (TRP) without quota for foreigners who align with traditional Russian values. Available to citizens of 47 countries (including US, UK, Canada, most EU states — list approved Sep 17, 2024 by PM Mishustin). No Russian language test or history exam required. Path to PR and citizenship follows standard TRP route.

programs.name: Highly Qualified Specialist (HQS) Work Permit
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals with specialized skills; minimum quarterly salary of RUB 750,000 (effective March 1, 2024); employer must file with Ministry of Internal Affairs
programs.processing_time: 14–30 business days; exempt from standard labor quotas
programs.notes: Work permit valid up to 3 years (duration of contract); unlimited extensions without leaving Russia. Employer-sponsored; employer submits application to MVD with employment contract.

programs.name: Skilled Worker Visa (New Pathway, effective April 15, 2026)
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals in science, economics, industry, education, culture, business, or sports; no Russian language test required; no immigration quota
programs.notes: Presidential Decree No. 883 (December 2025). Effective April 15, 2026. Eligible candidates can obtain 3-year temporary residency or apply directly for Permanent Residency. Application submitted through authorized agency digitally from home country; approved applicants receive 1-year business visa with immediate work rights. Family members included.

agreements: EAEU (Eurasian Economic Union) free movement of labor — Russia, Armenia, Belarus, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan
CIS (Commonwealth of Independent States) open borders/freedom of movement provisions

outbound_official.notes: Official source: Consular Information Portal of the Russian MFA (kdmid.ru), page titled 'Information about visa/visa-free travel regime for citizens with various passport types established through international agreements and unilateral decisions', as of 2026-05-08. Lists regimes for ordinary passport (общегражданский паспорт) holders. Russia's unified e-visa (single-entry, 30-day stay, 120-day validity, USD 52 fee) covers ~68 countries per electronic-visa.kdmid.ru. visa_on_arrival is empty — Russia does not operate a general visa-on-arrival outbound programme (these are destination-side VOA schemes). Some countries appear in both visa_free and e_visa where e-visa remains available as an alternative pathway. Albania, Algeria, Benin, Bulgaria, Bahrain listed as visa-free may apply only to diplomatic/service passports for ordinary-passport holders — full ordinary-passport verification was limited by page truncation; cross-referenced from existing visa_policy field in this file.

validation_notes: CORRECTED: China (CHN) in visa_free updated from '15 days for organized group tours only' to '30 days individual travel' per Presidential Executive Order signed Dec 1, 2025 (in force Dec 1, 2025 – Sep 14, 2026). Group tour visa-free (15 days, bilateral treaty) remains as fallback.

CORRECTED: Jordan (JOR) added to visa_free. Jordan-Russia bilateral visa-free agreement for ordinary passports entered into force Dec 13, 2025 (30 days per visit, max 90 days/year). Jordan was previously only in e_visa.
CORRECTED: Macao SAR max_stay_days updated from 14 to 30 days per current official information.
CORRECTED: Bolivia (BOL) source_url fixed — was incorrectly pointing to uae.mid.ru; corrected to electronic-visa.kdmid.ru.

CORRECTED: North Korea (PRK) REMOVED from e_visa list. North Korea did not appear in the official kdmid.ru country.html list of 68 e-visa eligible countries, nor in any official Russian government source consulted. Inclusion in prior record was an error.

CORRECTED: RuID/electronic registration note in visa_policy.notes updated — prior record stated registration is mandatory from June 30, 2025. Verified: registration is VOLUNTARY during experiment period until Dec 31, 2027 per Russian Ministry of Digital Development official clarifications.
CORRECTED: E-visa country count in notes updated from '66 countries' to '68 countries' per current official kdmid.ru portal.
ADDED: Georgia (GEO) added to visa_free with unlimited stay; CIS bilateral arrangement confirmed by multiple corroborating sources.
UPDATED: Iran (IRN) notes clarified — group-tours-only condition confirmed. Individual travelers from Iran must use e_visa (30-day, available on kdmid.ru portal).
NOTE: Lebanon diplomatic/service passport agreement (April 22, 2026) noted as signed but NOT yet in force.
gaps: China (CHN) individual visa-free (Dec 2025 executive order) expires Sep 14, 2026 — record will need updating after that date; it reverts to group tours only or a new permanent agreement.

The complete official visa-free country list with specific max-stay days per bilateral agreement could not be directly scraped from mid.ru due to connection errors — the visa-free entries are cross-referenced from mid.ru sub-domains and corroborated by multiple official Russian embassy sources but individual bilateral treaty texts were not retrieved.
Ukraine is listed in some sources as having visa-free access but given current geopolitical situation the status is unclear; omitted.

Abkhazia and South Ossetia visa-free entries omitted as these are contested territories not universally recognized; their status is based on Russian government declarations but not verifiable from a neutral official source.

Georgia (GEO) unlimited-stay visa-free was sourced from secondary sources (gw2ru.com) — direct official Russian MFA page for Georgia could not be fetched; treat as medium-confidence until verified from mid.ru directly.
The 'Shared Values Visa' list of 47 eligible countries was not independently verified from an official government domain.

Golden Visa (Decree No. 2573) min_amount shown is the lowest threshold (RUB 15M for socially significant projects); full investment criteria details are from secondary legal sources, not the decree text itself.
Russia does not have a digital nomad visa or ETA scheme as of available information.
The new Skilled Visa (Presidential Decree No. 883, April 2026) was verified via multiple immigration news sources referencing kremlin.ru but the kremlin.ru page itself timed out during direct access.
Visa-on-arrival: Russia does not operate a general visa-on-arrival program; omitted.

CONDITIONAL ACCESS — Alaska indigenous (Chukotka): The special visa-free arrangement for Alaska indigenous residents visiting Chukotka was not verifiable from a live official Russian government domain; sourced from Wikipedia. Treat as low-confidence.
CONDITIONAL ACCESS — Diplomatic/service passport holders (general): Full official per-country list could not be fetched from mid.ru directly. Entry sourced from Wikipedia.
CONDITIONAL ACCESS — Lebanon diplomatic passport waiver (April 22, 2026): Agreement signed but not yet in force; specific stay limits not published.
CONDITIONAL ACCESS — No OCI cardholder exemption found: Russia does not appear to offer any special conditional entry benefit for India OCI cardholders based on OCI status alone.

CONDITIONAL ACCESS — No US/UK/Schengen visa-based entry confirmed: Some aggregators claimed holders of valid US or Schengen multiple-entry visas can enter Russia visa-free for 15 days, but no official Russian government domain confirmed this rule. Not recorded.
Macao SAR max_stay updated to 30 days but could not be verified from live hongkong.mid.ru (connection failed); sourced from secondary sources.

North Korea e-visa eligibility: North Korea NOT on official kdmid.ru list but relationship with Russia has deepened significantly since 2024; possibility of special bilateral arrangement cannot be excluded — needs monitoring.
